certification.title: An AI workshop, backed by the 10xDevs certificate
certification.intro: I completed the cohort-based 10xDevs course — an intensive program on building software with AI agents, led by Przemek Smyrdek and Marcin Czarkowski. Below: the certificate, the badges I earned, and the five areas the course covered.

credential.issuer: BRAVE / 10xDEVS · Przemek Smyrdek and Marcin Czarkowski
credential.facts: Prework and 25 lessons across 5 modules, 6 live sessions (kick-off, 4× Live Q&A, celebration) plus weekly office hours. Final project: a custom AI agent and internal tooling built end-to-end.

builder.name: 10xBuilder
builder.caption: For building a working solution during the course.

architect.name: 10xArchitect
architect.caption: For designing architecture geared to working with AI agents.

champion.name: 10xChampion
champion.caption: A distinction for activity and results across the whole cohort.

bestProject.name: Best Project
bestProject.caption: A distinction for the final project.

1.title: Agentic Environment
1.desc: Setting up the environment for working with AI agents: tools, permissions and rules that keep the agent within the bounds of the project.

2.title: 10xDevs Workflow
2.desc: A repeatable track from brief to working code — with a clear split between what the agent does and where the human decides.

3.title: AI Development Quality & Maintenance
3.desc: Quality and maintenance of AI-built code: tests, reviews and standards that keep a project in good shape for years.

4.title: Innovation with generative AI
4.desc: Using generative AI to build new features and tools that genuinely speed up the work.

5.title: Large and legacy projects
5.desc: Working with AI in large codebases and legacy projects: making changes safely where the stakes are high.

hero.badgeSub: 4+ years of commercial development. I work with AI agents every day.
hero.headingHighlight: I work with AI every day
hero.sub: I ship commercial code in Flutter and Unity. Over the last year I rebuilt my workflow around AI agents — Claude Code with my own configuration, hooks and skills sits in my projects every day. I'm looking for a role or contract where this acceleration matters.
hero.ctaPrimary: Let's talk about a role or project

trust.yearsCode: Production code in Flutter (Dart) and Unity (C#)
trust.claudeDaily: Claude Code: my own config, hooks, skills, subagents
trust.engineerEnglish: BSc in Computer Science · Technical English B2/C1

howIWork.title: How I work — AI in my daily workflow
howIWork.intro: Four concrete areas where AI sits in my daily toolkit. It's not about pasting questions into a chat — it's a full workshop: agents in a loop, custom tools, hooks that enforce project standards, automations around review and CI.

21.title: AI-assisted development as a daily practice

1.lead: Claude Code as a daily driver
1.rest: — I use it for most programming tasks: implementation, debugging, exploring unfamiliar codebases, refactoring.

2.rest: — settings, permissions, custom slash commands, skills and subagents tailored to my projects.

3.lead: Working with the agent in a loop
3.rest: — planning the task, delegating exploration to subagents, verifying output, iterating. Not "chat" — a controlled workflow.

4.rest: — I know when an LLM is wrong, how to detect it, and how to set up the process so errors don't leak into production.

22.title: Agents and scripts supporting the SDLC

1.rest: — code reviewer, code explorer, planner, tester. Each with a different system prompt and a different tool scope.

2.rest: — enforcing conventions, blocking risky operations, automatic formatting/linting before commits.

3.rest: — using off-the-shelf MCP servers to integrate with team tools.

4.rest: — they capture repeatable team workflows (PR review, generating tests, preparing release notes).

1.rest: — the agent reads CI logs, suggests the cause, sometimes proposes a fix.

2.rest: — generating readable release notes / changelogs from diffs and commits.

3.rest: — a first layer of checks (style, obvious bugs, missing tests) before a human sees it.

4.rest: — bumping dependencies, generating scaffolds, codemods, large refactors with verification.

1.rest: — with a clear verification step (tests, type checks, manual review) before merge.

2.rest: — following project conventions, not in a "whatever" style.

3.rest: — large-scale code changes driven by the agent, with tests pinning behavior.

4.rest: — the agent doesn't merge on its own; every output goes through review and the build pipeline. AI accelerates, it doesn't replace responsibility.

whatICanDeliver.title: What I can deliver
whatICanDeliver.intro: First, what I have documented experience in. AI tooling and business processes — below, as areas I'm actively expanding into.

bullets.1: Android + iOS mobile apps from scratch and growing existing ones,
bullets.2: responsive and scalable UI, custom widgets, asset optimization,
bullets.3: state management with Riverpod, Freezed, Dependency Injection, Clean Architecture,
bullets.4: REST API and Firebase integrations, auth, sessions, offline-first data,
bullets.5: debugging on physical devices, publishing to Google Play / App Store.

unity.title: Interactive apps and games — Unity / C#

bullets.1: games and interactive tools, UI Toolkit / Canvas / responsive UI,
bullets.2: Firebase, REST API, Unity IAP integrations,
bullets.3: design patterns: State Machine, Pooling, Scriptable Object,
bullets.4: separating MonoBehaviour from pure C# logic, attention to readability and standards,
bullets.5: building editor tools and designer-facing tooling.

bullets.1: code readability, architectural scalability, long-term maintainability,
bullets.2: the full product lifecycle: planning → implementation → testing → release → maintenance,
bullets.3: collaboration with developers, testers, project managers, clients.

32.title: AI tooling in the developer workflow

bullets.1: Claude Code: configuration, hooks, slash commands, skills, subagents,
bullets.3: prompt engineering in a code context (structured output, function calling, tool use),
bullets.4: working with LLM APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic) at the application integration level,
bullets.6: evaluating output quality (where to trust the agent, where to require verification).

32.callout: I deliberately don't write "enterprise AI agents at scale" here — I work with these tools daily and have concrete artifacts, but I'm not pretending to have production rollouts for external clients behind me.

bullets.1: self-direction and project ownership — from brief to maintenance,
bullets.2: communication with the client, team, project manager,
bullets.3: working under time pressure and across many projects in parallel,
bullets.5: technical English B2/C1 — meetings, documentation, code review.

bullets.1: business process automation with an LLM layer (n8n, Make),
bullets.3: RAG over knowledge bases, vector DB, embeddings.

bullets.1: 14 years of prior work in low-voltage installations, CCTV, alarms, smart home,
bullets.2: electrical qualifications SEP E + D, driving license cat. B,
bullets.3: useful for projects that combine software with sensors, controllers, installations.

currentlyLearning.title: Growth
currentlyLearning.intro: A short list of topics I'm actively going deeper on — mostly to extend my agent toolkit beyond pure production code. I'm signaling direction, not selling it as a service.

agents.lead: Agents specialized for specific SDLC processes
agents.rest: automatic triage, generating regression tests, codemods at a larger scale.

rag.lead: RAG pipelines over closed knowledge bases
rag.rest: mainly aimed at internal project documentation.

automation.lead: Business process automation with an LLM layer
automation.rest: n8n, Make.com — I know the tools by name and am just starting to explore them.

currentlyLearning.closing: I test some of this on my own projects. If one of these areas is on your team's roadmap — you've come to the right place.

about.title: About me
about.short: A developer with 4+ years of commercial experience (Flutter, Unity) for whom AI is a workshop, not a curiosity. Focused on shipping software — faster and cleaner thanks to tools I configure myself. Previously 14 years in technical installations — useful for projects that bridge software and hardware.
about.para1: Most of my commercial work has been Flutter mobile apps (Honeti) — from the first line of code, through REST API and Firebase integrations, auth, data sync and offline work, all the way to store releases and maintenance. I've also worked in Unity on games and interactive apps. In both stacks I put strong emphasis on code readability, architectural scalability, and making sure the project still lends itself to development years in.
about.para2: Over the last year my workflow has gone through a noticeable shift. I started treating AI agents as a workshop tool, not a curiosity. Claude Code became my daily driver — with configuration tailored to my projects. Things I used to do by hand (review, exploring unfamiliar codebases, codemods, scaffold) now happen faster, but still under my control — the agent doesn't merge on its own, every output goes through build and review.
about.notSpecialist: I don't call myself an "AI Specialist".
about.selfDefinition: I call myself a developer who deliberately uses AI in daily work — and who, thanks to that, ships more.
about.difference: That's the distinction I care about: my track record is in writing production code — AI is the way that code is now produced faster.
about.para4: Before I started programming professionally, I worked for 14 years as an electrician and installer of CCTV, alarm, fire safety and smart home systems. For most software projects this background is neutral. On projects that bridge software and the physical world — IoT, smart buildings, automation — it gives me a perspective a pure developer doesn't have.
about.para5: I value end-to-end work: from analyzing the problem, through implementation, to maintenance. I don't leave projects in a "PoC" state. I don't promise things I can't deliver.

wsb.name: Wyższa Szkoła Biznesu — National Louis University, Nowy Sącz
wsb.title: BSc in Engineering

footer.tagline: Flutter · Unity · AI-Augmented Mobile Developer. Open to roles and contracts.

portfolio.title: Portfolio
portfolio.intro: Three competence tracks: commercial mobile apps built at Honeti, my own agentic-programming projects, and an AI workshop — the methodology I bring to every team.

honeti.title: Commercial portfolio —
honeti.honetiLinkAria: HONETi (opens honeti.com in a new tab)
honeti.role-line: As a Mobile Developer at Honeti — full lifecycle from planning through implementation, testing, and release to long-term maintenance.
honeti.cta-full-list: See the full portfolio for HONETi
honeti.subpage-lead: Working at Honeti was the full developer flow — discovery workshops and conversations with the client, feature prototyping, UX/UI design in a team, implementation, code review, testing, store releases, and post-launch maintenance. Every project below went through exactly that cycle — from the first meetings to the version that shipped to end users.

flutterSeries.title: Mobile apps — Android and iOS
flutterSeries.description: Projects delivered end-to-end — from design and client alignment, through prototyping, implementation, and testing, to store releases and post-launch maintenance. Full ownership of the mobile side.

contribution.signIn: Sign in with Google and Sign in with Apple

educationalSeries.title: Educational series
educationalSeries.description: An educational series for children built on a shared learning-mode engine — 4 apps from scratch (Angielski — słówka i obrazki, Czasowniki nieregularne IQ, Der Die Das, Flagi) plus 3 taken over for maintenance (Tabliczka Mnożenia, Potęgi i Pierwiastki, Cyfry Rzymskie).

agent.title: Agentic programming — first-party deployments
agent.intro: AI is part of my production process. Four live projects where you can see it in practice.

homeStorage.title: Neatu Storage
homeStorage.description: A mobile app for managing home inventory: it tracks items by storage location (pantry, fridge, freezer), monitors stock levels, scans barcodes, and builds shopping lists from what's running low.

neatuDashboard.title: Neatu Dashboard
neatuDashboard.description: The web companion of the Neatu ecosystem (neatu.app) — reach your inventory from the browser, alongside the mobile app.

polilocale.title: Polylocale
polilocale.description: My open-source localization tool. Format-faithful architecture, AI translation through the user's own API keys.

buzzards.title: buzzards-soft.com
buzzards.description: The site you are reading, built with the active assistance of AI agents. The entire process is publicly documented.

warsztat.title: AI Native Development Workflow
warsztat.intro: The same controlled track from idea to deployment — with a clear split between what we do together, what the agent does on its own, and where a human decides.

flow.loopback: If the review catches something — we go back to "Context and plan" for new decisions.

plan.title: Context and plan
plan.desc: Together we settle the full scope of the change: the goal, the area in the code, dependencies, and the order of steps. Project context — conventions, decisions, architecture — is written down and fed to the agent. A concrete plan with clear scope boundaries emerges.

accept.title: Plan approval
accept.desc: The agent presents the finished plan and I go through it point by point — approving each step or correcting the scope. Code only starts after my approval.

agent.label: Agent at work · unsupervised
agent.loop: build ⇄ test ⇄ check · until everything passes

build.title: Build
build.desc: The agent writes code and unit tests.

review.title: Automated check
review.desc: The agent reviews the whole thing and writes up a list of remarks.

humanReview.title: Assessment and decision
humanReview.desc: I read the collected remarks and the summary from the automated check, then decide: approve the work, order fixes, or reject it. The judgment of quality and fit to the plan stays with the human.

live.title: Live testing
live.desc: I check the look and behavior on a real device — catching what only shows up in the finished product. Fixes and polish we do together, until everything works and looks the way it should ship.

deploy.title: Deployment
deploy.desc: Shared work built on CI/CD. The agent prepares the change description, saves it on a separate branch, and opens a pull request. An autonomous agent wired into the repository reviews the changes on its own. The final approval, merge, and deployment are done by me.

toolkit.lead: What stands behind this track — the methodical foundation and the concrete tools that keep the pace without losing control.

spec.title: Spec-Driven Development
spec.desc: Nothing is built before a written, approved specification. The plan is the contract, and design decisions are recorded ahead of time — before any code exists.

context.title: Context-Driven Development
context.desc: The agent works from durable, versioned project context — conventions, decisions, and memory from earlier sessions. Every task starts from the same, agreed foundation.

sessions.title: Specialized agent sessions
sessions.desc: A separate session for planning, building, testing, and review — each with its own tools, prompt, and a single task to deliver.

tools.title: Real tools in the stack
tools.desc: Live library documentation, checking the look in the browser, end-to-end tests on a mobile device. The agent works with real tools in my stack.

commands.title: Repeatable processes as commands
commands.desc: Software-development steps wrapped into ready commands the whole team runs — from planning to reviewing changes.

git.title: Control over what lands in the repository
git.desc: The agent works on a separate branch and opens a pull request at my request. A clear split: what the agent does, what I do, and how it ties into CI/CD.

hero.title: Sprint-driven workflow with typed sessions
hero.description: Every change starts from durable context — BRIEF.md, a sprint file, or an ADR — not from a fresh prompt. Every agent session has a type (planner / implementer / tester / reviewer) and a single declared deliverable. Reviewer sessions are read-only — they cannot edit code, only write a list of concerns. The result: faster, but still disciplined — the agent does not merge on its own.

1.title: Claude Code configuration
1.description: Per-project settings.json with permissions, custom keybindings, hookify rules with a concrete hook, custom status line / powerline.

2.title: Specialized sub-agents
2.description: code-reviewer, sprint-implementer, sprint-tester, sprint-reviewer, codex-rescue. Each sub-agent has its own tools, prompt, and scope — not one universal agent that does everything.

3.title: Custom slash commands and skills
3.description: Repeatable SDLC processes packed into commands the whole team can run — from sprint planning to PR review.

4.title: MCP integrations in workflow
4.description: context7 (live library docs), Playwright (browser UI verification), Maestro (mobile e2e). The agent has real tools in my stack, not just knowledge.

5.title: Persistent memory + bilingual rule
5.description: Memory system with types (user / feedback / project / reference) + a PL-conversation / EN-markdown meta-convention enforced by the workflow itself. Context does not vanish between sessions.

6.title: Full control over my git workflow
6.description: The agent works on a worktree or a feature branch, never directly on main. It only opens a PR when I ask. The split is planned: what the agent does, what I do, how it ties into CI/CD. Full control over what lands in git.
6.example: worktree · feature branch · PR on demand

privacyPolicy.metaDescription: Rules for processing personal data of users of buzzards-soft.com — in line with GDPR.
privacyPolicy.title: Privacy policy
privacyPolicy.introPart1Pre: This Privacy Policy sets out the rules for processing and protecting the personal data of users accessing the website at
privacyPolicy.introPart2: The Controller takes particular care to protect users' privacy and the personal data being processed in accordance with applicable law, in particular Regulation (EU) 2016/679 of the European Parliament and of the Council (GDPR).
privacyPolicy.adminContactBody: Buzzards Soft Mariusz Jendrzejczak, Pomorska 14/8, 74-300 Myślibórz, Poland.

1.title: Scope of collected data
1.intro: While using the Site, the Controller may process the following data:

items.1: data provided voluntarily by the user (e.g. via the contact form): full name, email address, message content,
items.2: technical data: IP address, browser type, operating system, statistical information about Site usage.

1.extra: The Site does not collect sensitive data.

2.title: Purposes of data processing
2.intro: Personal data is processed for the following purposes:

items.1: handling inquiries submitted via the contact form,
items.3: ensuring the proper operation of the Site,

3.title: Legal basis for processing
3.intro: Personal data is processed on the basis of:

items.2: the legitimate interests of the Controller (Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR),

4.title: Data recipients
4.p1: Users' personal data may be passed to entities cooperating with the Controller solely to the extent necessary to achieve the processing purposes, in particular: hosting providers, analytics tools, and IT services.
4.p2: Data is not sold or shared with third parties for marketing purposes.

5.title: Data retention
5.p1: Personal data is stored only for the period necessary to achieve the purpose for which it was collected, or until the user withdraws consent, unless further processing is required by law.

6.title: User rights
6.intro: The user has the right to:

items.3: erase it ("the right to be forgotten"),

6.extra: To exercise these rights, the user may contact the Controller using the address provided in section 10.

7.title: Cookies
7.intro: The Site may use cookies for:

items.1: ensuring the proper operation of the Site,

7.extra: The user may change cookie settings in their web browser at any time.

8.title: Data security
8.p1: The Controller applies appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect the personal data being processed against loss, unauthorized access, or unauthorized modification.

9.title: Changes to the privacy policy
9.p1: The Controller reserves the right to make changes to this Privacy Policy. The current version is always available on the Site, and the date of the last update is shown in the header.

10.title: Contact
10.p1: For matters related to the protection of personal data, please contact us by email. We usually respond within a few business days.

meta.title: Websites for businesses and freelancers — Buzzards Soft
meta.description: A professional website designed around your business and handed over to you: the code, the content and the accounts are all in your name. I work with AI, so you get a finished site faster and cheaper, while a real person watches over every step and stands behind the quality.

hero.eyebrow: Websites for small businesses and freelancers
hero.heading: A professional website designed around your business — and it's yours to own.
hero.subheading: I design it around your business and hand it over to you: the code, the content and the accounts are all in your name. I work with AI, so you get a finished site faster and cheaper, while a real person watches over every step, stands behind the quality and guides you all the way to launch.
hero.ctaPrimary: Get a quote for your site

problem.heading: Who this offer is for
problem.body1: This offer is mainly for small and micro-businesses, freelancers, and private individuals or institutions who want a clean, simple website on a sensible budget. If that sounds like you, you're in the right place.
problem.body2: I bring two worlds together here. You're in direct contact with me, so at every step you know what's happening, can ask questions and request changes. At the same time I use AI tools that speed up my work and let me keep the price friendly.
problem.body3: My goal is to deliver a good, well-finished website to every client, whatever the scale of the project. Experience with this technology lets me do that for businesses that, before the AI era, often couldn't afford a build like this.

guide.heading: How I work on your website
guide.intro: I work with AI and I say so openly. The tools speed up part of the work, but I lead the whole thing and I'm accountable for the result — at every stage you know what's happening and have a real say in what gets built. Here's the full path from idea to finished site:

conversation.title: Conversation
conversation.body: We start with a plain, human conversation in everyday language. You tell me what you do and what the site should give you. Together we settle the scope, the style and a rough timeline, and we split the tasks: what you provide and what I take on. You leave that conversation with a clear picture of what will be built and what it costs.

mockup.title: Live mockup
mockup.body: I build the mockup straight away as a real, clickable page on sample content. You view the actual layout in your browser like a finished product: section placement, colours, spacing, proportions. That way you see something concrete right from the start and can react while a change of direction is still easy and quick. I apply your notes before we move into the real content.

content.title: Content and materials
content.body: We gather content in parallel with the mockup. Copy, logo and photos are, as a rule, yours to provide — it's your company and your voice. If something is missing, we work through it together and find the best route: from help polishing the copy, through AI graphics and stock, to a trusted designer or UX designer when you need your own logo or a look designed from scratch. That last option is a separate, paid service with a specialist, and I pull it all together into one coherent site.

build.title: Build and testing
build.body: Once the layout and content are approved, I take the whole thing to the finish and polish every detail. I mind the details that decide quality: loading speed and correct display on phone, tablet and computer. I test everything so the site looks good and runs reliably.

review.title: Review and refinement
review.body: I hand the finished site over for you to look at. You click, read, check it on your own phone and tell me plainly what fits and what to refine. The number of rounds depends on the package (usually two to four), so you know up front how much room you have for changes and when the site is done. Those rounds are part of the service — that's where we refine the site.

publish.title: Hosting, domain and launch
publish.body: Finally I handle the whole technical side. I configure hosting, connect the domain (and if you need one, I help you choose and buy it) and publish the site online, on your accounts. From that moment the site is live and starts working for your business.

proof.heading: The best proof is right in front of you.
proof.body: This site you're reading right now was built with exactly the process I sell — the same person, the same tools, the same review stages. Want to look deeper? I describe the whole workshop step by step.

includes.heading: Here's what you get with every site
includes.intro: Whichever package and scope you choose, you get the same solid set of essentials:

items.mobile: A fast site that works on every phone — and that's where most of your customers will come from.
items.contact: A contact form and one-tap phone call, so customers reach out right away.
items.seo: A technical SEO foundation, so Google sees you from day one.
items.editing: A simple panel to update your content — texts and images — yourself, anytime, while I keep the look polished and consistent.
items.hosting: Free hosting, your own domain and an SSL certificate — everything ready to launch.
items.gdpr: GDPR compliance: a cookie banner and privacy policy, so you can operate with peace of mind.
items.content: Copy prepared and checked with my process, if you don't have your own.
items.support: Care and consultation at every stage of the build. I'm available throughout: you ask, I advise, and we make decisions together, so you always know where things stand. I guide you through the process in plain language, so every choice is yours and fully informed.
items.security: A secure, encrypted connection (SSL) and hosting on Google's infrastructure. Data between your site and your visitors travels encrypted, and the browser shows the padlock that builds instant trust. Your site runs on the same proven backend the big services rely on.

pricing.heading: Simple packages, transparent prices
pricing.promoNote: price valid until the end of August
pricing.footnote: Net prices. The promotional price applies until the end of August.

rowLabels.aiContent: AI copy, checked with my process
rowLabels.base: Form, technical SEO, GDPR, hosting and domain

full.name: Full
full.audience: a business that wants to be visible and win leads
full.extras: Local visibility (Google Business), Animations, Dark and light mode
full.extrasValue: Separately these modules would be +1050 zł

custom.name: Bigger project
custom.audience: shop, custom build from scratch, complex integrations

modules.heading: Extend your site with what you need

leads.name: Lead capture (mailing list or a submissions sheet)

ownership.heading: The site is yours to own
ownership.body: I put the domain, hosting, code, content and accounts in your name and on your accounts. You pay once — the only recurring cost is your own domain (around 50–90 zł a year, paid to the registrar). If you ever wanted to move to someone else, you take the whole thing and carry on.
ownership.limitsHeading: I stand behind all of this — and I tell you where the limits are

seo.lead: I build a solid technical foundation for SEO and local visibility.
seo.body: I don't run ongoing ranking campaigns or guarantee first place on Google — that's a separate, continuous service, and I'll honestly point you to who to hire for it.

commerce.lead: Shops, complex integrations and a real CRM system I handle as a custom quote.
commerce.body: They don't fit into the Basic or Full package, so I price them separately, for the specific project.

duration.question: How long does it take?
duration.answer: Usually from a few days to about 2 weeks, depending on the package.

materials.question: What materials do I need to prepare?
materials.answer: As a rule, the materials (copy, logo and photos) are yours to provide, since it's your company and your brand. If something is missing, we find a solution together. I'll tidy up and polish your copy so it reads professionally. Graphics are a separate field, so you can source a logo and photos three ways: an AI design, paid stock photos, or commissioning a trusted specialist as an add-on service. Each path is a cost on your side, and I'll help you choose the one that fits your budget.

selfEdit.question: Will I be able to edit the site myself later?
selfEdit.answer: During the build you have revision rounds included. After launch you update your content — texts and images — yourself, anytime, in the panel that comes with every site. Larger changes beyond content, like new subpages or features, I price by scope.

security.question: Is my site secure?
security.answer: Yes. The site runs over an encrypted HTTPS connection, so form data and visitor traffic are protected, and the browser shows a padlock in the address bar. Hosting sits on Google's infrastructure — the same backend that powers large sites.

gdpr.question: What about GDPR?
gdpr.answer: A cookie banner and privacy policy come with every site.

quote.heading: Tell me briefly what you need, and I'll get back to you with a quote.
quote.intro: No commitment. Write a few sentences about what you do and what you need, and I'll reply with a quote and a suggestion for how to do it.
quote.messagePlaceholder: what business or service you have and what you need
